title: Example consent-first registry schema (governance, synthetic only)
context: Data governance and consent-first registry design are important skills for cancer data professionals. Teaching these concepts requires a schema/governance example. Per PLAN.md and the binding cancer guardrails, this artifact is explicitly: schema/governance examples only, populated with synthetic or deceased/aggregate data only, consent-first framing, and never a live patient data store. Risk tier is medium because schema design for patient data registries involves legal/ethical framing that requires careful language — any ambiguity that implies real patient data could be stored must be flagged. The backlog note specifies 'Schema/governance example; synthetic/aggregate only; never a live store'.
objective: Produce a consent-first registry schema and governance document as a teaching example, explicitly scoped to schema/governance education only — never a live patient data store — with synthetic/aggregate-only sample data.
acceptanceCriteria: Document is explicitly framed as a schema/governance teaching example: 'This is a teaching artifact for understanding registry design — not a live patient data store, not real patient data, not medical advice.'
Registry schema covers: consent record fields, data subject rights (access, withdrawal), data use limitations, governance roles, audit trail fields, and retention policy example.
Any sample data included is synthetic or from deceased/aggregate publicly-available sources; no real patient records.
Explicitly addresses: consent-first design (no data without consent record), data minimization, purpose limitation, and breach notification process as schema-level constraints.
Safety notice present; riskTier:medium; document does not imply fitness for regulatory compliance without legal review.
Document committed to the project repo; commit DCO signed-off; outputLicense: CC-BY-4.0.
resources: GDPR Article 5 (data minimization, purpose limitation) as educational reference
GA4GH Data Use Ontology (educational reference for consent categories)
output: A consent-first registry schema + governance document as a teaching example, explicitly framed as schema/governance education only (never a live store), committed to the project repo.
